{"query":"Sanae Takaichi Donald Trump G7 June 2026 meeting joint statement Indo-Pacific","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1428,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://www.japantimes.co.jp/news/2026/06/17/japan/politics/takaichi-trump-g7-china-indopacific/","title":"Takaichi and Trump talk Indo-Pacific as Mideast crisis overshadows G7 summit - The Japan Times","content":"Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi held brief talks with U.S.
President Donald Trump on the sidelines of the Group of Seven (G7) summit in France on Tuesday, exchanging views on the Indo-Pacific even as the crisis in the Middle East continued to dominate the gathering."},{"url":"https://japan.kantei.go.jp/105/speech/202606/0613kaiken.html","title":"Prime Minister Takaichi Spoke to the Press Regarding Her Upcoming Visit to the United Kingdom, Italy and France | Speeches | Prime Minister's Office of Japan","content":"I will shortly depart for the United Kingdom and Italy, and then travel to France to attend the G7 Evian Summit.
I previously held summit meetings with Prime Minister Starmer of the United Kingdom and Prime Minister Meloni of Italy during their visits to Japan.
This time, based on the latest developments, I would like to exchange views on issues such as the situation in the Middle East, the situation in Ukraine, and developments in East Asia.
As the leader advocating for Asia, I intend to actively communicate Japan's position and initiatives, including from the perspective of the Indo-Pacific."},{"url":"https://www.mofa.go.jp/na/na1/us/pageite_000001_01689.html","title":"Prime Minister Takaichi’s Talk with the U.S. President Trump | Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan","content":"Prime Minister Takaichi welcomed the recent agreement of a Memorandum of Understanding between the United States and Iran.
The two leaders exchanged views on the situation in the Indo-Pacific region, including China.
The two leaders confirmed that they would continue to maintain close communication in responding to developments in the international situation in the Indo-Pacific and the Middle East."},{"url":"https://mainichi.jp/english/articles/20260618/p2g/00m/0na/038000c","title":"Takaichi keeps Japan's quiet diplomacy on course at G7 - The Mainichi","content":"GENEVA (Kyodo) -- Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi returned home from her debut at the Group of Seven summit this week, having reinforced Japan's reputation as a reliable and steady member, while gaining more than visibility as the group's only Asian member.
President Donald Trump or French President Emmanuel Macron, she did not set the tone of discussions or dominate the political narrative.
Japanese priorities were reflected, for example, in the topic-specific joint statements issued over the three days of talks in the French resort town of Evian-les-Bains, near Geneva.
The joint statement issued by the G7 leaders on Ukraine, the Middle East and the Indo-Pacific was "unexpected" both because it was issued and because of the context in which it was released, said Stephen Nagy, a professor at International Christian University in Tokyo.
For Tokyo, Ukraine is closely tied to Asian security."},{"url":"https://www.arabnews.jp/en/japan/article_172371/","title":"Japan PM Takaichi: do not believe U.S. interest towards Indo-Pacific is declining｜Arab News Japan","content":"EVIAN-LES-BAINS (France): Japanese Prime Minister TAKAICHI ​Sanae said on Wednesday she did not believe that ‌U.S. ‌interest ​towards ‌the ⁠Indo-Pacific ​region was declining.
President Donald ‌Trump, Japan confirmed it ⁠would continue close ⁠coordination on the situation in the Indo-Pacific, including China."}]}

Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi and U.S. President Donald Trump hold a brief meeting on the sidelines of the G7 Summit in France, discussing the U.S.-Iran agreement, Japan-U.S. trade, and security in the Indo-Pacific.
